items.title: 'Unrealistic' to Expect Warsh To Bring About Cuts Anytime Soon Says Smart
items.summary: Christopher Smart, managing partner at Arbroath Group and a former Obama administration official, joined Balance of Power to discuss the jobs report and economic risks from the Middle East war. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
items.summary: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

items.title: IAEA : UAE nuclear plant attack  serious compromise of nuclear safety
US Hiring Surged in May, Boosting Bets on Fed Rate Hike
items.summary:
US job growth topped all forecasts in May and the unemployment rate held steady at¬†4.3%, offering the clearest sign yet that the labor market may be breaking out of a prolonged period of lackluster hiring.
Frances Donald, Chief Economist at RBC, discusses the jobs data and what it signals for the US economy as inflation begins to outpace wage growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

Citigroup Economists Maintain Lonely Call for Fed Rate Cuts
items.summary: Unswayed by strong US jobs data released Friday, economists at Citigroup Inc. are maintaining their increasingly lonely prediction that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates three times this year.

Unfortunately, the Russian side once again chooses war ‚Äď everyone heard the response today. Weak response. He simply does not want to end the war.

I think many around the world were disappointed by that response. He does not want to change anything, and he does not want to admit that this war appeals only to him ‚Äď and to those who are making money off him. They were all smiling very broadly today.

That means Russia must have less money, and there must be more pressure on Russia.

I thank everyone who is helping us. I thank everyone who stands with Ukraine and wants a real peace.
